title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for intermediaries, hop-by-hop fields, forwarding, and transformation and requires the smallest strict intermediary forwarding and transformation contract with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for intermediary forwarding and transformation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hop By Hop Removal
steps.instruction:
Order the Hop By Hop Removal evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hop By Hop Removal.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Hop By Hop Removal assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Hop By Hop Removal, keep Hop By Hop Removal connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A quiz protocol team handles case 1297 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Hop By Hop Removal has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Intermediary Failure Ownership
steps.instruction:
